# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-07-20

Claude Code now ships as a Bun runtime compiled with Rust rather than its previous Node-based distribution.

Went through 37 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
   Claude Code is core daily tooling; a runtime and packaging change can silently break wrappers, version pins, and container builds.

2. Speech Recognition and TTS in less than 500kb (Moonshine Micro)
   On-device speech at this footprint would remove a recurring per-minute API cost from any voice-agent product.

3. Our AI bill for June: $3,000
   Concrete, self-reported inference spend is a rare and directly comparable cost datapoint.

4. Setting up your spare Mac for Claude Code to control, a step-by-step guide
   Directly applicable to multi-agent workflows, and the dedicated-machine pattern meaningfully limits blast radius.

5. Transcribe.cpp
   Local transcription removes a recurring API cost and a privacy exposure for audio workloads.

Full brief with all 37 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-07-20

Draft status: not approved for posting.
